w:r.w:t: of the NextGen Test Platform. Its job is to

— ingest every source of truth about the application and organize it so downstream layers (Test Design, Generation, Execution, Analysis) never have to guess or hallucinate. Per the architecture document, Layer 1 has
— full automation (webhooks, crawlers) comes in Stage 2. Focus is on creating the structured stores and basic APIs that enable Layer 3 (Generation) to be grounded.
Manual + admin-curated population. Basic text embedding for requirements.
Jira webhook, nightly UI crawler, PR-triggered re-ingestion
Full schema + admin CRUD + seed data for InvoiceAgility demo
Vector index + Structured store with defects/incidents/test cases
Real-time RAG retrieval at scale, graph queries
Code path linkage via static analysis
Activates when a new requirement or update enters the system. Parses, chunks, embeds, and crawls relevant sources to keep the App Model and Knowledge Base current.
Instead of full automation (webhooks, crawlers), Stage 1 builds the
via admin actions. The engine has the right interfaces so Stage 2 can swap in automated triggers without changing downstream logic.
Accept a requirement (text or structured), extract key entities, generate embeddings, store in Vector Index
Use the LLM to extract: input fields, numeric ranges, business rules, state transitions, performance SLAs, integrations
(or Azure equivalent, depending on Baxter's approved AI services)
Vector store (pgvector recommended) — store embedding + raw text + extracted entities
Ingest a new requirement (called from UI when user enters requirement)
Ingest/update App Model for a project (admin upload structured JSON)
Add a defect/incident record to Knowledge Base (manual entry for now)
— a structured, machine-readable representation of every interface surface of the application. Per the architecture doc:
"Everything downstream is grounded here, which is exactly what stops selector hallucination."
— every API endpoint with request/response schemas
— every UI page with verified locators
-- Parent table — one App Model per project, versioned
Get the active App Model for a project (returns all sub-stores)
"PO number prefix 'CO' → route invoice to Coupa"
"Numeric PO number → route invoice to JDE"
"Invoice image sent as URL link to ERP, NOT downloaded image"
"Multiple buyers sharing same VAT + address = uncertain classification"
"All invoices go to Verify queue before export (Process Control = Always)"
"Rejected invoices stay in rejected queue for 12 months then auto-delete"
"This is what generation and triage retrieve from (RAG), so the AI reasons over your reality rather than generic priors."
"anything related to moving funds between accounts?"
returns REQ-2847 even if the query phrasing differs.
-- adjust dim based on model (1536 for ada-002, 1536 for text-embedding-3-small)
-- Store B: Gold Standard Test Cases (also embedded in Vector Index)
-- full test case in Output Template format
Semantic search across vector index. Body:
Get defects, optionally filtered by component
Get requirements related to a given one
Add a defect (manual entry in Stage 1)
Mark a test case as gold standard
Retrieve gold standard test cases by similarity
"Invoice export fails silently when PO number contains leading zeros"
"When PO starts with '0', system strips the zero and routing to JDE fails"
"Buyer classification incorrect when multiple buyers share VAT and address"
"System auto-assigns to first matching buyer instead of flagging for manual review"
"MuleSoft polling timeout during month-end invoice surge"
"GetOutputDocuments API timed out under load on month-end day, causing export backlog"
"API pagination not implemented; single response with 5000+ documents"
that ties requirements ↔ test cases ↔ code paths ↔ defects. Per architecture:
"It answers two questions cheaply: 'Is this requirement tested?' and 'If this code changes, which tests must rerun?'"
✅ Requirement ↔ Test Cases linkage (auto-populated when test cases are generated)
✅ Requirement ↔ Defects linkage (manual + auto on defect creation)
⏸️ Requirement ↔ Code Paths linkage (Stage 2 — requires static analysis)
-- STAGE 2 STUB: Code path linkages (table created, but population deferred)
Get full matrix for a project
Get matrix row for a specific requirement
Link test cases to a requirement (auto-called by Generation Layer)
"Export invoices from AP Essentials to JDE/Coupa based on PO prefix"
Create all DB migration scripts for 4 sub-components
Build App Model CRUD APIs (all 3 sub-stores)
Postman tests for all endpoints pass
Build Requirement Ingestion endpoint with entity extraction + embedding
Ingest sample requirement → see embedding stored, entities extracted
Build Vector Index + semantic search endpoint
Query "invoice routing" → returns relevant defects/test cases
Seed IVA defects → retrievable by component filter
Link a test case → coverage % auto-updates
Wire up: Generation Layer (existing) consumes App Model via API
Test generation now includes real IVA entities (e.g.,
Wire up: Generation Layer auto-updates Traceability Matrix
Build admin panel views for: App Model, KB Defects, Traceability
E2E demo dry-run with InvoiceAgility scenario
Seeded: App Model (full), 2 Defects, 1 Incident, 3 Gold Standard test cases
— Open admin panel → display API Contracts, POM, Schema Graph, Business Rules. Highlight: real IVA entities like
— Display DEF-IVA-001 (PO leading zeros), INC-IVA-001 (month-end timeout). Run semantic search "invoice export issues" → both returned
"Test the invoice export process to ensure PO numbers with leading zeros are correctly routed to JDE"
Semantic similarity finds DEF-IVA-001 (high relevance) → injected as risk signal
Generate Test Cases (Layer 3 — already built, now grounded)
Pulls App Model → uses real field names
Queries KB → finds DEF-IVA-001 → elevates priority of leading-zeros test case to P1
Uses Gold Standard test cases as few-shot examples
— Auto-populated: requirement linked to 12 generated test cases, linked to DEF-IVA-001 (regression coverage). Coverage shows 0% → 75%
🟡 60% (manual triggers, infrastructure in place for Stage 2)
🟢 95% (full schema + seed data, missing UI crawler)
